data.narrative:
Aalyria, a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Chris Taylor, has raised $150M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $1.3B. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of 8 private peers at the same or adjacent stage—including Axiom Space ($2.5B, Series C), Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B, Series B), Impulse Space ($1.8B, Series C), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B, Series C), and Skyryse ($1.1B, Series C)—alongside 5 publicly traded Space & Aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL). Public comps trade at a median of 64.2x revenue.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, implying $2.5B. Peer-Median methodology yields $1.3B, drawing from the 8-company sample with a range of $400M to $2.5B. Public-Comp anchoring is not applicable due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, common among private companies.

These methods triangulate to a median of $1.9B, with a low bound of $1.1B and a high bound of $2.8B. The current $1.3B valuation sits near the peer median but below the stage-progression implied value. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue run rate and capital efficiency relative to peers, both critical for validation against public multiples once disclosed.
